rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid type resolution errors.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in appropriate annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current scope or declared locally.
Validate that helper declarations match the types of the expressions they define.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for boolean helper declarations and `const` for constants with explicit scalar types.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assumptions and bindings there.
Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL scope or declared in the same annex.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package references
